last_item: Band-2.5 (resource & config safety): disk headroom alert — warn well before the data/WAL volume fills.
last_result: done (iter 25) — added an early-warning disk tier `disk-headroom-low` (WARNING, host.disk_percent >= 80%, For:5m, Cooldown:1h) to alert.DefaultRules(), sitting below the existing CRITICAL `disk-almost-full` (90%). 90% is the emergency; this gives the operator runway to act (prune WAL, grow the volume, archive) before a slow fill can stop Postgres. Mirrors the backup-stale+backup-failed two-tier pattern. No new metric/wiring: MetricDiskPercent (a statfs of the PG data/WAL volume) and the seed/Collector.SampleOnce->Engine.Evaluate->dispatch loop already exist; the background.go seed inserts only MISSING rule IDs, so an upgrading panel gets the new rule without clobbering operator edits. 5-min For window ignores a transient bump (e.g. a deep restore-test scratch copy on the same volume); 1h cooldown re-reminds without a firehose; above 90% both tiers independently active (intended escalation). Tests: updated TestDefaultRules want-list + new TestDiskHeadroomEarlyWarningTier proving tier ordering (warning lower-severity AND lower-threshold than critical) and non-vacuous firing (at 85% disk the warning fires after its For window while critical stays OK). Proven non-vacuous (warning threshold->95% fails the ordering assertion). Go gate green (gofmt/vet/test/build, sandbox-disabled per snap); web/ untouched. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no blocking issues; fixed one misleading inline comment it flagged. Confirmed no other test asserts a hardcoded default-rule count (background_test uses len(DefaultRules())).
notes: BAND 1.5 + BAND 2 COMPLETE. IN BAND 2.5 (resource & config safety). DONE iter 25: disk-headroom-low early-warning rule (WARNING 80% on host.disk_percent, For 5m/Cooldown 1h) added below the critical disk-almost-full (90%) in alert.DefaultRules(). KEY: adding a disk/conn alert is usually RULE-ONLY — Snapshot already has disk_used/total_bytes + connections + max_connections, alert/metrics.go metricValue computes host.disk_percent / pg.connections_percent, and background.go's seedDefaultAlertRules inserts only missing IDs (idempotent, never clobbers operator edits) so a new DefaultRules() entry auto-seeds on upgrade. Test pattern: extend TestDefaultRules want-list + a focused engine test (saveRule + eng.Evaluate at a chosen snapshot, advance time past For to fire). background_test asserts len(DefaultRules()) dynamically so count is safe. REMAINING band-2.5 open items: (b) SELF-HEALING config — a postgresql.conf change (default OR user override) that stops PG auto-rolls-back to last-known-good, PG never left down (test: bad setting -> rollback -> PG up); this is the big one, needs real config-apply + restart-probe + rollback logic. (c) host-sized tuning at provision (shared_buffers/work_mem/effective_cache_size/max_connections from detected RAM/CPU per DEFAULTS.md; test the sizing fn against sample RAM values) — pure function, easy to test, no PG needed. (d) connection-saturation alert as active conns approach max_connections — likely RULE-ONLY like (a): connections-near-max ALREADY EXISTS in DefaultRules() (WARNING pg.connections_percent >= 85, For 2m). So (d) may already be SATISFIED — audit it; if so, delete the backlog item with a one-line reason, or add an early/critical tier if a single 85% warning is judged insufficient. --- Alert plumbing recap: Collector.SampleOnce -> Engine.Evaluate -> dispatchAlertEvents (background.go); metricValue (alert/metrics.go) reads Snapshot fields; go is a snap -> run go/psql/pg_ctl sandbox-disabled; empty $TMPDIR in some shells -> use /tmp. --- HISTORICAL: band 1.5 (backups proven-restorable, S3 nudge, restore-test verify+deep, scheduled jobs) and band 2 (web silent-failure audit, idempotent provisioning, query-box auto-LIMIT+statement_timeout enforced) all COMPLETE. See git log for details.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
